@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubearmor-1186-securing-kubearmor-pods-and-policies-policing-the-police",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubearmor: securing KubeArmor pods and policies (Policing the Police!)",
+    "description": "securing KubeArmor pods and policies (Policing the Police!). Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kubearmor deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kubearmor version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor\nhelm list -n kubearmor 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kubearmor installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubearmor configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubearmor configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor\nkubectl get configmap -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubearmor\n```\nKubeArmor is a security engine and thus it is imperative that it follows all the security best practices. The aim is to ensure security of the KubeArmor itself."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for securing KubeArmor pods and policies (Policing the Police!)",
+        "description": "- How would KubeArmor daemonset attach a profile to itself?\n  - Failed admission if annotation added and no profile found\n\nPotential Solution\n- Snitch has the intelligence to load the default apparmor profile.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubearmor -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubearmor\nkubectl get events -n kubearmor --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"securing KubeArmor pods and policies (Policing the Police!)\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "- How would KubeArmor daemonset attach a profile to itself?\n  - Failed admission if annotation added and no profile found\n\nPotential Solution\n- Snitch has the intelligence to load the default apparmor profile.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
